headline: In heatwave, some French museums and monuments become cool havens while others are forced to close
description: Several cultural venues have transformed into climate refuges by offering free admission, while others have had to stop welcoming visitors due to the extreme heat.

headline: 250 years of American independence: A groundbreaking Constitution for 'a more perfect union'
Heatwave in Europe: From Spain to Germany, an unprecedented 'red zone' sweeps across continent
France detects first Ebola case in doctor returning from DRC
description: This is the first case ever diagnosed on French soil. Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu is monitoring the situation 'very closely,' according to his office, as quoted by Agence France-Presse.

headline: The unavoidable rise of a less American world
description: The war in the Middle East has sped up a realignment that was already underway, forcing the US's partners to rethink flexible alliances without creating overly strong dependencies.

headline: Resistance fighter Marc Bloch inducted into Panthéon in moving moment of republican solidarity
description: On Tuesday, the tribute ceremony recounted the life of the historian, who was executed by the Germans on June 16, 1944. In a speech that served as an indictment against Vichy France and antisemitism, President Emmanuel Macron lamented the 'spirit of defeat' that, he said, permeates 'our public life.'

headline: UN commission denounces ongoing 'genocide' in Gaza: 'By targeting children, Israel is attacking the very capacity of the Palestinian people to exist'
description: In a report published Tuesday, an international commission of inquiry, mandated by the United Nations Human Rights Council, identified Israeli security force units that it accused of being responsible for the deaths of Palestinian children. Israel condemned the report as 'defamatory.'

France's ambiguous memory of slavery
Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
description: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

headline: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
description: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.

headline: France's public pools need urgent renovation: 'What would we do without them? We wouldn't make it'
description: Aging, energy-intensive and expensive for rural towns to maintain, many aquatic centers in the countryside have been shutting down. Yet these facilities provide equal access for people to cool off and learn to swim. An MP recently sounded the alarm about the worrying situation facing these essential local public services.

headline: Heeding Resistance fighter Marc Bloch's warning against 'strange defeats'
Flagrant unpreparedness to heatwaves highlights urgent need for climate anticipation in France
'Marc Bloch was persecuted not only by the Nazi occupiers, but also by the French state'
